In a vote of confidence for Meta’s Threads, Kalshi adds sharing feature
articles.description: Kalshi now offers a share option that will automatically embed the relevant prediction market chart into a Threads post.

articles.title: Google and Tesla think we’re managing the electrical grid all wrong
articles.description: The duo have teamed up with other companies to launch Utilize, which seeks to change how the grid is used and regulated.

articles.title: Amazon launches its healthcare AI assistant on its website and app
articles.description: Health AI can answer questions, explain health records, manage prescription renewals, book appointments, and more.

articles.title: DOGE employee stole Social Security data and put it on a thumb drive, report says
articles.description: A whistleblower is accusing a former DOGE member of stealing a large number of Americans’ personal data while he was working at the Social Security Administration, with the plan of using it at his new job.

articles.title: ‘Pokémon Pokopia’ is a game about rehabilitating a broken world — and I love it
articles.description: The latest Pokémon game is a cozy life simulator like "Animal Crossing" or "Minecraft," but in a way that feels more grounded to our actual world.

articles.title: YouTube surpasses Disney, Paramount, WBD in 2025 ad revenue
articles.description: This shift is a clear sign of just how much content consumption has changed over the years and how advertisers have adapted their strategies.

articles.title: AI-powered apps struggle with long-term retention, new report shows
articles.description:
AI can drive stronger early
monetization for apps, but sustaining value remains the challenge, RevenueCat's latest report finds.

articles.title: Mandiant’s founder just raised $190M for his autonomous AI agent security startup
articles.description: Kevin Mandia founded Armadin to create autonomous cybersecurity agents, software designed to learn and respond to threats without a human in the middle.

articles.title: ChatGPT can now create interactive visuals to help you understand math and science concepts
articles.description: Instead of just reading an explanation or looking at a static diagram, users can now engage directly with interactive visuals.

articles.title: US military contractor likely built iPhone hacking tools used by Russian spies in Ukraine
articles.description: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.

articles.title: AgentMail raises $6M to build an email service for AI agents
articles.description: AgentMail provides an API platform that lets you give AI agents their own email inboxes, with support for two-way conversations, parsing, threading, labeling, searching, and replying.

articles.title: Thinking Machines Lab inks massive compute deal with Nvidia
articles.description: The multi-year deal involves at least a gigawatt of compute power and also includes a strategic investment from Nvidia.

articles.title: Google gives in to users’ complaints over AI-powered ‘Ask Photos’ search feature
articles.description: The option appears on the Google Photos Search screen and lets users pick which experience they want.

articles.title: Legora reaches $5.55 billion valuation as AI legal tech boom endures
articles.description: Legora, an AI platform for lawyers, is now valued at $5.55 billion following a $550 million Series D led by Accel to fuel its growth in the U.S.

articles.title: Meta acquired Moltbook, the AI agent social network that went viral because of fake posts
articles.description: Meta says that Moltbook's approach to "connecting agents through an always-on-directory" is novel.

articles.title: YouTube expands AI deepfake detection to politicians, government officials, and journalists
articles.description: YouTube's AI deepfake detection tool is becoming available to politicians, journalists, and officials, letting them flag unauthorized likenesses for removal.

articles.title: Adobe is debuting an AI assistant for Photoshop
articles.description: Adobe is also adding new AI-powered image-editing features to Firefly.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack
URL: https://infinity.inc/case-studies/qwen3-optimization
Generated code is now outperforming hand-tuned inference libraries
Infinity claims to have beaten vLLM—the current gold standard for LLM serving—using an automatically generated inference stack optimized for Qwen3. If reproducible, this suggests the meta-game in AI infrastructure is shifting from "build the best kernel" to "build the best kernel generator." Watch for vLLM's response and independent benchmarks.

### Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon
URL: https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/rcli
YC backs a bet that Apple Silicon becomes a serious inference platform
RunAnywhere targets the growing fleet of M-series Macs sitting underutilized in enterprises. The play here is clear: if you can't get H100 allocation, your employees' laptops become your inference cluster. Interesting timing given Nvidia's supply constraints and Apple's aggressive chip roadmap.

### Unions merged into dotnet 11 preview 3
URL: https://github.com/dotnet/csharplang/blob/main/proposals/unions.md
C# finally gets discriminated unions after years of community demand
This closes a major expressiveness gap between C# and F#/Rust/Swift. For enterprise shops locked into the .NET ecosystem, this is a meaningful quality-of-life improvement that will affect how data modeling happens in large codebases. Expect library authors to start leveraging this immediately.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### Meta acquired Moltbook, the AI agent social network that went viral because of fake posts
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/10/meta-acquired-moltbook-the-ai-agent-social-network-that-went-viral-because-of-fake-posts/
Meta is buying its way into agent-to-agent infrastructure
Meta's rationale—an "always-on directory" for agents—signals they see agent networking as the next platform battle. The fact that Moltbook's virality came from fake posts is a feature, not a bug, for a company betting that AI-generated content will dominate feeds. This is an acqui-hire plus strategic positioning, not a product play.

### Thinking Machines Lab inks massive compute deal with Nvidia
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/10/thinking-machines-lab-inks-massive-compute-deal-with-nvidia/
A gigawatt of compute signals Nvidia is picking winners in the lab race
Multi-year, gigawatt-scale deals with strategic investment attached are Nvidia's way of creating moats around favored partners. The scale here—roughly 100,000+ H100 equivalents running continuously—puts Thinking Machines in a very small club. Worth tracking who else gets these deals and who doesn't.

### Legora reaches $5.55 billion valuation as AI legal tech boom endures
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/10/legora-reaches-5-55-billion-valuation-as-ai-legaltech-boom-endures/
Legal AI is the first vertical where AI valuations are holding
$550M at $5.55B is a 10x entry for Accel—aggressive in this market. Legal has clear unit economics: $500+/hour labor being automated. This valuation implies Legora is either generating serious revenue or Accel believes winner-take-most dynamics are emerging. Compare to horizontal AI plays trading at steep discounts.

### Mandiant's founder just raised $190M for his autonomous AI agent security startup
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/10/mandiants-founder-just-raised-190m-for-his-autonomous-ai-agent-security-startup/
Cybersecurity's best operator is betting on autonomous defense
Kevin Mandia built Mandiant into a $5.4B exit to Google. His thesis at Armadin—that security needs agents responding without human latency—is a direct counter to the "human in the loop" orthodoxy. At $190M, this is likely a $1B+ post, suggesting investors see autonomous security as inevitable despite the liability questions.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world
URL: https://www.wired.com/story/yann-lecun-raises-dollar1-billion-to-build-ai-that-understands-the-physical-world/
The world-model contrarian finally has capital to prove his thesis
LeCun has been publicly critical of the autoregressive scaling paradigm for years. A billion dollars lets him actually build the alternative—AI that learns physics and causality rather than token prediction. If he's right, current frontier labs are optimizing the wrong objective. If he's wrong, this is an expensive intellectual vanity project.

### TLA+ as a Design Accelerator: Lessons from the Industry
URL: http://muratbuffalo.blogspot.com/2026/03/tla-as-design-accelerator-lessons-from.html
Formal methods are quietly winning in production systems
Murat Demirbas has been tracking TLA+ adoption for years; this post aggregates real industry lessons. The pattern: teams using formal specification find bugs earlier and ship faster, not slower. AWS, Azure, and MongoDB have all published on this. For anyone building distributed systems, this is the evidence base for making the investment.

### AI-powered apps struggle with long-term retention, new report shows
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/10/ai-powered-apps-struggle-with-long-term-retention-new-report-shows/
RevenueCat data shows AI apps monetize fast but churn faster
This is the data that should worry every AI-first startup. Strong early monetization masks a retention problem that compounds monthly. The implication: AI features drive trial conversion but not habit formation. Winners will be apps that use AI to enhance sticky workflows, not apps that are "AI" as the value prop.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The throughline today is infrastructure consolidation meeting vertical specialization. Nvidia is kingmaking labs through compute allocation while Meta buys agent infrastructure and LeCun raises enough to build a parallel research agenda. Meanwhile, the real money is flowing to verticals—legal, security, healthcare—where AI solves specific, expensive problems rather than generic ones. The retention data on AI apps is the reality check: the horizontal "AI wrapper" play is dying, but purpose-built AI for high-value workflows is just getting started.
